author: PC Gamer
text:
I got my hands on Framework's 'MacBook Pro for Linux users' and its tagline isn't just marketing hyperbole
The Framework 13 Pro is a sleek, stylish machine lazer-focused on software devs sick of MacOS.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The blower is back: Cooler Master has made a GPU accessory that pushes hot air outside your case rather than onto your CPU
This thing sits atop of your graphics card and offers a 4–6 degree improvement, says Cooler Master.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Cooler Master is playing around with a 2000 W CPU and GPU cooling solution with four huge 180 mm fans
Project AIO Liquid Cooling Solution.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Final Fantasy 14 seasonal events schedule: what to expect and when to expect them
All your go-to events under one roof.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Legendary Warhammer artist John Blanche has died
Blanche's work set a stark, brutal vision for the grim dark future.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Which gaming clip just won $500? Check out the best videos of May, featuring nukes in Minecraft, eternal drifts in Forza Horizon 6, and a dragon-riding giant in Skyrim
You sent in some amazing gaming videos in May. Here are our favorites, plus the winner of a $500 gift card.

author: PC Gamer
text:
007 First Light sequels will be 'done by MGM and, theoretically, by Amazon Game Studios,' Amazon exec says
IO Interactive self-published 007 First Light, but don't expect that to happen with future releases.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Summer Game Fest is here, so get ready for a lot of, 'Ugh, that game with the cool trailer used AI'
A pile of new game announcements are coming over the next several days, and we can expect a pile of AI disclosures, too.

author: PC Gamer
text:
868-Back makes hacking as cool as '90s Hollywood thought it was
I just had to hack my own brain to get Back.

author: PC Gamer
text:
They did motion capture on a cube
God of War Laufey necessitated an exciting new form of method acting.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This is me playing Alan Wake 2's native Arm build on an RTX Spark laptop, and I'm here for it
Remarkably, playing Pragmata and Indiana Jones via Prism's emulation wasn't really any different.

author: PC Gamer
text:
For the sixth year running, Dontnod honors Pride Month by giving away an award-winning adventure game
Tell Me Why is free on Steam and Xbox for the duration of June.

author: PC Gamer
text:
So long suckers! I am leaving to drink heavily and repair cheap cars in Poland
In the '90s, specifically.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Corsair announces solid wood front panels for its Frame 5000D PC cases, including a ripple one I'm not entirely sure about
It's a bit trippy.

author: PC Gamer
text:
PlayerUnknown's survival game is shutting down 6 months after launch: 'I have reached the limits of how far I can continue to fund this journey'
PlayerUnknown Productions, founded by Brendan Greene after he left Krafton in 2021, is also laying off staff.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I made treants awkwardly rub shoulders with ringwraiths in the Total War-style RTS roguelike Tabletop Tavern, and I'm surprised as anybody that it worked
It takes all sorts.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Computex 2026 has really highlighted the fact that PC gaming tech isn't all about gaming anymore
Companies seem to be looking to de-niche.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Marathon's new PvE experiment is Bungie's most genius decision yet, so make it a full mode already
Explore the swamp in relative peace.

author: PC Gamer
text:
What's better than one high tier gaming rig? A double-decker gaming rig, according to Thermaltake
Yo dawg.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This new microATX PC case comes with a Venom-like black liquid that reacts to sound and magnets, and I find it weirdly alluring
A case with a different kind of screen.

author: PC Gamer
text:
NAND flash makers earned a record $46 billion in revenues over the first quarter of 2026, a shocking 3.5 times more than last year
Gosh, I wonder how? Was it AI, by any chance?

author: PC Gamer
text:
We got eyes on Ducky's sleek Year of the Horse gaming keyboard, and I can say I like it more than its last horse one
Though the lack of lettering looks like a bit of a mare.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The action-RPG our editor was excited enough to fly to China for just got a 50-day delay
Phantom Blade, uh, 50. Days. Sorry, I should've thought of a joke before I started writing.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Bungie's traditional launch woes made for a terrible first impression of Marathon's exciting second season and undermined its free-play week
How do all those anteaters, monkeys, or weasels even fit in Bungie HQ?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Cherry attempts to change my mind about wireless gaming keyboards with world's first Ultra-Wideband button basher
A clacking good time?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sticking two fingers up at the economy, Biwin's new Origin Code memory sticks are as extreme as you could imagine, with up to 256 GB of DDR5-8000 CL42
Plus an EXPO ULL kit of DDR5-6000 CL26, though none of it is what you'd call affordable.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How to watch the PC Gaming Show 2026, including Exodus extended gameplay reveal highlights
Here are all the details on when and where the PC Gaming Show premieres this year from worldwide times to streaming platforms.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Morrowind modder returns to remake a 20-year-old epic—a beloved 'Discworld, not Monty Python' project that's 'unexpectedly lore-friendly'
Rise and shine.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Thermaltake has built a PSU that splits in two… on purpose
All in the name of an easier installation process.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Corsair's new and supposedly affordable seat continues 'the move away from traditional gaming chair style'
I'm liking the vision for this one.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Remember when Star Wars and Command & Conquer ruled PC gaming? June in PC Gamer 10, 20, and 30 years ago
Here's what was happening in PC gaming in June 1996, 2006 and 2016.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I regret to inform you that you almost certainly overlooked a sick as hell shrimp game on Steam last month
There's still time to correct this grievous error.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'There is no world where it makes sense,' Warframe creative director says of Destiny 2's demise: 'It's unthinkable'
Rebb Ford says Destiny 2 is a victim of businessmen.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A Persona 6 announcement may be incoming
Supposed leaked images have reportedly been taken down due to copyright complaints.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Everyone in Rust is getting a new face
Player models are getting overhauled for the first time in a decade.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'We have a great relationship with IO Interactive,' Amazon says, but it won't commit to bringing the studio back for future Bond games
Amazon says "it’s still too early to discuss future projects."

author: PC Gamer
text:
'What was unravelling is suddenly stabilizing': Destiny 2's last update is getting an absurd 71 pages of patch notes, but its story is closing with a shrug
At least that big, beautiful orb is back where it belongs.

author: PC Gamer
text:
One mad lad has spent the past 3 years remixing hundreds of classic metal songs in the Doom midi soundfont
Hell yeah dude.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Guy who swatted Grand Theft Auto 5 star is going to prison for 4 years: 'Won't be swatting anyone anytime soon... and they're coming for the ones he ratted out'
Ned Luke, who portrayed Michael De Santa in GTA 5, has been swatted multiple times over the past three years.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Elden Ring's promised update with new classes and armor will finally be ours in August⁠—but we still don't know if it's paid or free
I guess it also comes with a Switch 2 version.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Path of Exile 2 raises the bar for all other action RPGs, and it's not even finished yet
The newest update redefines what an endgame can be and cements PoE 2 as one of the most ambitious action RPGs I've ever played.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Gothic Remake launch times and release date in your region
The Nameless Hero returns (without a name still).

author: PC Gamer
text:
Summer Game Fest 2026 preview: What to expect from this year's showcases
What's confirmed, and what might show up at Summer Game Fest, the Xbox Games Showcase, and the PC Gaming Show this year.

author: PC Gamer
text:
We could get a Resident Evil game set in Japan someday as producer Masato Kumazawa reveals the development team 'has given it some thought'
Who could say no to a Resident Evil game set in Japan?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Montech's new shuttered PC case is so odd, I can't decide whether I'm missing the sheer brilliance of it or if it really is totally pointless
Two words for you: fan and profile.

author: PC Gamer
text:
MSI somehow has both the ugliest and prettiest laptop designs this Computex
You win some and you lose some.

author: PC Gamer
text:
New report claims Intel is 'struggling' to supply laptop CPUs based on its latest 18A node after speaking to Computex sources
But it might not be because there's a problem with 18A itself.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Subnautica 2 promises to add 'one of the most requested' features, but sorry, it's not a knife you can stab fish with
You can't get stabby, but you can "get stompy."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Corsair's slightly-outrageous Warthog PC case is the thing I'd most like to take home from Computex 2026, and I'm not ashamed to say it
Shove one in your luggage for me, would you?

author: PC Gamer
text:
I thought I was over 2.5D, but make it look like Marvel vs. Capcom plus Berserk and I'm back, baby
Reject chunky pixels, embrace fluid animation.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The best quiet gaming keyboard we've tried is getting a smaller TKL version this year
Here's hoping the price is lighter.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Corsair's range-topping AX1600i power supply gets some much-needed upgrades, including pin monitoring, proper GPU power sockets, and a reduction in size
The hammer blow of the price tag has yet to fall, though.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This year's Computex has highlighted just how big an impact Apple can have on the wider computing industry
Now let's hope the industry follows the MacBook Neo's pricing.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This skyscraper-shaped custom PC at Computex has a food stall out the front selling 'Taiwanese sausage with sticky rice' and a rock climber free soloing up the side
You've gotta hand it to them.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Ace Combat's developers have been making games about fighter jets for 30 years, but they've never gotten to fly in one
Anyone know how to rent an F-16 for a weekend?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Where to find Edogawa Baseball Stadium in Forza Horizon 6: Pitcher Perfect photo challenge location
Snap a picture of the Edogawa baseball field.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The excess of Computex feels a little more tone deaf than usual this year, as the pricing elephant in the room becomes harder to ignore
It's all about the money.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The follow-up to one of my favorite RPGs of the 2020s finally has a demo, and it does not disappoint: Bat people, a mushroom hellscape, and funky JRPG combat from bizarro world 2005
Embracing Entropy.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I never change weapons in RPGs, which means I've made Obsidian director Josh Sawyer's life a living hell when it comes to making cool armour systems
Sorry, Josh.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Ace Combat director says 'I've been doing this for 30 years, so I know exactly how hard we're going'—and after playing it, I can confirm Ace Combat 8 goes extremely hard
Wings of Theve is more Ace Combat. Emphasis on the *more.*
